nodes.name: init_problem_context
nodes.description: Bootstraps immutable problem context for all later kernels: graph topology, lattice abstraction, lattice instance, previously generated subgraphs, and seed.

inputs.name: graph
inputs.constraints: Required; treated as immutable input state

outputs.name: problem_context
outputs.constraints: Immutable state object carrying graph, lattice, lattice_instance, previously_generated_subgraphs, seed

nodes.type_signature: (graph: Graph, lattice_instance: LatticeInstance, previously_generated_subgraphs: Collection[Subgraph], seed: int) -> GreedyMappingContext
nodes.conceptual_summary: Deterministic Immutable Context Bootstrap. Aggregates multiple heterogeneous structural inputs into a single immutable state carrier that is passed unchanged across later computational kernels. The transform performs no optimization or inference; it formalizes boundary conditions by binding a read-only relational topology, a discrete embedding/placement scaffold context, historical substructure constraints, and a reproducibility token into one canonical context object. This creates a stable reference frame so downstream greedy or stochastic procedures can be evaluated consistently.. Applications: Initializing a molecular conformation search pipeline with a fixed bond graph, spatial discretization schema, prior sampled motifs, and a random seed, Bootstrapping robotic path-planning with workspace connectivity, occupancy grid instance, previously explored trajectory fragments, and deterministic rollout seed, Preparing a distributed scheduling optimizer using system dependency topology, resource-slot lattice instance, prior allocation patterns, and reproducible simulation seed

nodes.name: construct_mapping_state_via_greedy_expansion
nodes.description: Builds and evolves immutable mapping state: initialize seed placement, score candidate graph nodes greedily, and extend mapping/frontier with feasible placements.

inputs.name: mapping_state_in
inputs.constraints: Immutable flow state containing mapping, unmapping, unexpanded_nodes, free_lattice_neighbors

outputs.name: scored_nodes
outputs.constraints: Greedy scores computed from graph/lattice context

nodes.type_signature: (problem_context: GreedyMappingContext, starting_node: NodeId, mapping_state_in: MappingState, considered_nodes: Collection[NodeId], remove_invalid_placement_nodes: bool, rank_nodes: bool) -> tuple[MappingState, Collection[ScoredNode]]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Immutable Greedy Partial-Embedding Expansion. Transforms an immutable partial assignment state by optionally seeding an initial assignment, then evaluating candidate elements with a local greedy scoring functional derived from a fixed structural context. Feasible candidates are selected and applied to produce a new immutable state with updated assigned/unassigned sets and expansion frontier. Optional pruning removes infeasible candidates, and optional ranking controls whether scored candidates are ordered for downstream selection.. Applications: Incremental assembly of molecular substructures onto admissible spatial sites using local fit scores and steric feasibility constraints., Progressive placement of software tasks onto heterogeneous compute units using heuristic utility scoring and resource-compatibility filtering., Stepwise expansion of robot exploration waypoints on a discretized workspace using frontier scoring and collision-feasibility pruning., Iterative assignment of logical components to hardware fabric positions in physical design using local cost ranking and legality checks.

nodes.name: seed_mapping_at_lattice_center
nodes.description: Compute the lattice start position from lattice size and return a new mapping state with the starting graph node placed and marked unexpanded.

inputs.name: problem_context
inputs.constraints: Must expose lattice with node count.

inputs.name: mapping_state_in
inputs.constraints: Immutable input state containing mapping, unmapping, unexpanded_nodes, frontier.

outputs.name: mapping_state_seeded
outputs.constraints: Same as input state, but with starting_node placed and added to unexpanded_nodes.

nodes.type_signature: (problem_context: GreedyMappingContext, starting_node: NodeId, mapping_state_in: MappingState) -> tuple[MappingState, NodeId]

nodes.name: select_unplaced_candidates
nodes.description: Filter considered nodes to only those not already mapped in the seeded state.

outputs.name: unplaced_nodes
outputs.constraints: Subset of considered_nodes not in mapping.

nodes.name: score_and_rank_candidates_greedily
nodes.description: Optionally call greedy_node_scoring and produce ranked unplaced nodes; also emit scored node records for downstream observability/output.

inputs.name: mapping_state_seeded
inputs.constraints: Provides current mapping for feature computation.

inputs.name: rank_nodes
inputs.constraints: If false, preserve original candidate order and emit empty/default scores.

outputs.name: ranked_unplaced_nodes
outputs.constraints: Sorted descending by (heuristic_score, random_tiebreaker) when rank_nodes=true.

outputs.name: scored_nodes
outputs.constraints: Node-level score payload derived from greedy_node_scoring.

nodes.type_signature: (problem_context: GreedyMappingContext, unplaced_nodes: Collection[NodeId], mapping_state_seeded: MappingState, remove_invalid_placement_nodes: bool, rank_nodes: bool) -> tuple[Collection[NodeId], Collection[ScoredNode]]

nodes.name: greedily_place_feasible_candidates_on_frontier
nodes.description: Iterate frontier lattice neighbors and greedily place the first candidate satisfying lattice/graph adjacency consistency checks, returning updated mapping state and leftovers.

inputs.name: problem_context
inputs.constraints: Must provide graph and lattice topology.

inputs.name: mapping_state_seeded
inputs.constraints: Must include mapping, unmapping, unexpanded_nodes, and free_lattice_neighbors frontier.

inputs.name: ranked_unplaced_nodes
inputs.constraints: Candidate nodes in greedy priority order.

outputs.name: mapping_state_extended
outputs.constraints: State after zero or more feasible placements, with unexpanded_nodes updated for placed nodes.

outputs.name: remaining_unplaced_nodes
outputs.constraints: Candidates still not placed after frontier scan.

nodes.type_signature: (problem_context: GreedyMappingContext, mapping_state_seeded: MappingState, ranked_unplaced_nodes: Collection[NodeId]) -> tuple[MappingState, Collection[NodeId]]

nodes.name: finalize_mapping_state_with_optional_pruning
nodes.description: Apply optional invalid-node pruning as a pure state transform and return final mapping state while forwarding scored nodes.

inputs.name: remaining_unplaced_nodes
inputs.constraints: Nodes eligible for pruning when enabled.

inputs.name: remove_invalid_placement_nodes
inputs.constraints: If true, output state reflects graph-with-removed-nodes; if false, graph unchanged.

outputs.name: mapping_state_out
outputs.constraints: Final immutable state including any pruning effect.

outputs.name: scored_nodes_out
outputs.constraints: Same logical payload as scored_nodes input.

nodes.type_signature: (mapping_state_extended: MappingState, remaining_unplaced_nodes: Collection[NodeId], remove_invalid_placement_nodes: bool, scored_nodes: Collection[ScoredNode]) -> tuple[MappingState, Collection[ScoredNode]]

nodes.name: orchestrate_generation_and_validate
nodes.description: Entry-point orchestration kernel (GreedyMapping): drives iterative expansion, invokes validity checks, and returns final generated subgraph mapping and inverse mapping.

inputs.name: mapping_state
inputs.constraints: Threaded immutable state from expansion kernel

outputs.name: is_valid
outputs.constraints: Result of final mapping validity check

nodes.type_signature: (problem_context: GreedyMappingContext, starting_node: NodeId, remove_invalid_placement_nodes: bool, rank_nodes: bool, mapping_state: MappingState) -> tuple[MappingState, bool]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Iterative Candidate-Expansion Orchestrator with Terminal Constraint Validation. Coordinates an iterative, greedy state-transition process over a discrete relational structure, starting from a required seed index and threading an immutable state snapshot through each expansion step. Runtime control flags conditionally route execution behavior for candidate pruning and ordering during expansion. After expansion terminates, a separate constraint-evaluation routine is applied to the resulting state, returning both the terminal state and a Boolean validity indicator.. Applications: Compiler optimization: incrementally assigning intermediate-representation nodes to target instruction patterns with final legality checks, Robotics task planning: greedy assembly of action-to-resource bindings with terminal feasibility validation, Molecular design workflows: stepwise construction of interaction mappings between structural motifs followed by rule-based consistency screening, Electronic design automation: iterative netlist-to-layout element assignment with post-generation design-rule verification

nodes.name: initialize_state_with_start_anchor
nodes.description: Create empty mapping containers, seed the starting graph node onto the lattice via `initialize_mapping`, and establish the current expansion pointers.

inputs.name: problem_context
inputs.constraints: Must provide graph, lattice, and initialize_mapping helper.

outputs.name: mapping_state
outputs.constraints: Contains mapping, unmapping, unexpanded_nodes, current_node, current_lattice_node initialized for first iteration.

nodes.name: greedy_expand_frontier
nodes.description: While unexpanded nodes remain, remove current node from frontier, derive free lattice neighbors and graph neighbors, call `extend_mapping_with_nodes`, and advance to the next unexpanded anchor.

inputs.name: problem_context
inputs.constraints: Must provide graph, lattice, and extend_mapping_with_nodes helper.

inputs.name: mapping_state
inputs.constraints: Must include valid current_node/current_lattice_node and mutable mapping sets.

outputs.name: expanded_mapping_state
outputs.constraints: Frontier exhausted (`unexpanded_nodes` empty) with accumulated mapping/unmapping.

nodes.type_signature: (problem_context: GreedyMappingContext, mapping_state: MappingState, remove_invalid_placement_nodes: bool, rank_nodes: bool) -> MappingState

nodes.name: assess_mapping_validity
nodes.description: Run `check_mapping_validity` to verify bidirectional adjacency consistency between graph and lattice projections.

inputs.name: expanded_mapping_state
inputs.constraints: Must include mapping and unmapping dictionaries.

outputs.name: is_valid
outputs.constraints: True only if all mapped adjacencies are consistent in both domains.

nodes.name: emit_final_generation_result
nodes.description: Package the expanded mapping state with validity status as the orchestration output contract.

inputs.name: expanded_mapping_state
inputs.constraints: Candidate final state after greedy expansion.

outputs.name: final_mapping_state
outputs.constraints: Returned mapping state for downstream use.

outputs.name: is_valid
outputs.constraints: Returned validity flag for caller-level decision.
